file_name: ClickstreamCorp_20200330_1-A_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_12089935_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_Development Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: The Developer hereby warrants and represents that following delivery of the Application System to the Client (which shall be deeded to occur only on the date the Web Application is uploaded to the AWS for distribution) pursuant to Exhibit A (the "Support Period"), the Application will be free from programming errors and defects in workmanship and materials, and will conform to the specifications of Exhibit A. If programming errors or other defects are discovered during the Support Period, the Developer shall promptly remedy those errors or defects at its own expense. The developer will fix any bugs that may come up from the original contract after the 90 days warranty has passed.
perturbation.changed_text: The Developer hereby warrants and represents that following delivery of the Application System to the Client (which shall be deeded to occur only on the date the Web Application is uploaded to the AWS for distribution) pursuant to Exhibit A (the "Support Period"), the Application will be free from programming errors and defects in workmanship and materials, and will conform to the specifications of Exhibit A. If programming errors or other defects are discovered during the Support Period, the Developer may, at their discretion, remedy those errors or defects, with costs to be negotiated. The developer will address high-priority bugs reported within the first 30 days after the 90 days warranty has passed, resources permitting.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity regarding the developer's obligation to fix errors. The original text states that the developer 'shall promptly remedy' errors at their own expense during the support period. The modification changes this to 'may, at their discretion, remedy those errors or defects, with costs to be negotiated' introducing uncertainty about whether errors will be fixed and who will bear the costs. This directly contradicts the earlier part of the sentence ensuring the application 'will be free from programming errors' and also the overall warranty, in Section 7, that the application will function as specified, creating an in-text contradiction. The modification about fixing bugs after the 90-day period also conflicts with the initial 90 day warranty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Developer hereby warrants and represents that following delivery of the Application System to the Client (which shall be deeded to occur only on the date the Web Application is uploaded to the AWS for distribution) pursuant to Exhibit A (the "Support Period"), the Application will be free from programming errors and defects in workmanship and materials, and will conform to the specifications of Exhibit A. If programming errors or other defects are discovered during the Support Period, the Developer may, at their discretion, remedy those errors or defects, with costs to be negotiated. The developer will address high-priority bugs reported within the first 30 days after the 90 days warranty has passed, resources permitting.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the developer's obligation to fix errors, shifting from 'shall promptly remedy' to 'may, at their discretion, remedy...with costs to be negotiated.' This introduces ambiguity about whether errors will be fixed and who will bear the costs, directly conflicting with the warranty stating the application 'will be free from programming errors' and creating an in-text contradiction. Furthermore, adding conditions after the warranty period, conflicts with the initial warranty.

perturbation.original_text: The Developer agrees to perform the Services hereunder solely as an independent contractor. The Parties agree that nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as creating a joint venture, partnership, franchise, agency, employer/employee, or similar relationship between the Parties, or as authorizing either Party to act as the agent of the other. The Developer is and will remain an independent contractor in its relationship to the Client. The Client shall not be responsible for withholding taxes with respect to the Developer's compensation hereunder. The Developer shall have no claim against the Client hereunder or otherwise for vacation pay, sick leave, retirement benefits, social security, worker's compensation, health or disability benefits, unemployment insurance benefits, or employee benefits of any kind. Nothing in this Agreement shall create any obligation between either Party or a third party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Developer agrees to perform the Services hereunder solely as an independent contractor, except where explicit client direction is required for project success. The Parties agree that nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as creating a joint venture, partnership, franchise, agency, employer/employee, or similar relationship between the Parties, or as authorizing either Party to act as the agent of the other, except where project deadlines necessitate direct client intervention. The Developer is and will remain an independent contractor in its relationship to the Client, unless deemed otherwise by mutual agreement documented in writing. The Client shall not be responsible for withholding taxes with respect to the Developer's compensation hereunder, except in cases of prolonged on-site work exceeding 30 days. The Developer shall have no claim against the Client hereunder or otherwise for vacation pay, sick leave, retirement benefits, social security, worker's compensation, health or disability benefits, unemployment insurance benefits, or employee benefits of any kind, except as required by law in jurisdictions where the Developer operates under explicit client direction. Nothing in this Agreement shall create any obligation between either Party or a third party.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces numerous exceptions to the independent contractor clause, blurring the lines between an independent contractor and an employee. The original text clearly defines the Developer as an independent contractor with no exceptions. The modified version introduces conditional clauses such as 'except where explicit client direction is required,' 'except where project deadlines necessitate direct client intervention,' 'unless deemed otherwise by mutual agreement,' and 'except in cases of prolonged on-site work exceeding 30 days'. These exceptions contradict the initial statement of independent contractor status and potentially trigger legal obligations typically associated with an employer/employee relationship. This contradicts the nature of relationship defined in the start of the section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Developer agrees to perform the Services hereunder solely as an independent contractor, except where explicit client direction is required for project success. The Parties agree that nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as creating a joint venture, partnership, franchise, agency, employer/employee, or similar relationship between the Parties, or as authorizing either Party to act as the agent of the other, except where project deadlines necessitate direct client intervention. The Developer is and will remain an independent contractor in its relationship to the Client, unless deemed otherwise by mutual agreement documented in writing. The Client shall not be responsible for withholding taxes with respect to the Developer's compensation hereunder, except in cases of prolonged on-site work exceeding 30 days. The Developer shall have no claim against the Client hereunder or otherwise for vacation pay, sick leave, retirement benefits, social security, worker's compensation, health or disability benefits, unemployment insurance benefits, or employee benefits of any kind, except as required by law in jurisdictions where the Developer operates under explicit client direction. Nothing in this Agreement shall create any obligation between either Party or a third party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several exceptions to the 'independent contractor' status, which directly contradicts the original, unqualified statement. Phrases like 'except where explicit client direction is required,' 'except where project deadlines necessitate direct client intervention,' and 'unless deemed otherwise by mutual agreement' create ambiguity and conflict with the initial definition, potentially leading to an employer/employee relationship. This directly impacts obligations related to taxes and benefits, contradicting the provisions stating the Client is not responsible for withholding taxes or providing employee benefits. The 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition applies as the changes introduce inconsistent terms, making enforcement difficult and interpretation unclear.

perturbation.original_text: The Developer shall not apply for, acquire, or claim any right, title, or interest in or to any such service marks, trademarks, trade names, Copyrights and Patents or others that may be confusingly similar to any of them, through advertising or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: The Developer shall not apply for, acquire, or claim any right, title, or interest in or to any such service marks, trademarks, trade names, Copyrights and Patents or others that may be confusingly similar to any of them, through advertising or otherwise, unless explicitly authorized by the Client in writing for specific co-branding initiatives.
perturbation.explanation: The original text prohibits the Developer from claiming any rights to the Client's intellectual property. The modified version introduces an exception: 'unless explicitly authorized by the Client in writing for specific co-branding initiatives.' This contradicts the initial absolute prohibition, potentially allowing the Developer to acquire rights to IP under certain conditions. This creates an in-text contradiction within the overall intent of the Intellectual Property section, which is to protect the Client's ownership.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Developer hereby represents and warrants that the use and proposed use of the Application by the Client or any third party does not and shall not infringe, and the Developer has not received any notice, complaint, threat, or claim alleging infringement of, any trademark, copyright, patent, trade secrets, industrial design, or other rights of any third party in the Application, and the use of the Application will not include any activity that may constitute "passing off." To the extent the Application infringes on the rights of any such third party, the Developer shall obtain a license or consent from such third party permitting the use of the Application. It is hereby stated, the Application developed under this agreement is the exclusive worldwide sole property of Client.
perturbation.justification: The original text imposes a strict prohibition on the Developer acquiring any rights to the Client's IP. The changed text introduces an exception allowing the Developer to acquire such rights with written authorization for co-branding, directly contradicting the original absolute prohibition. This conflicts with the 'Contradicted Text' which states 'the Application developed under this agreement is the exclusive worldwide sole property of Client', as the modification introduces an avenue where the Developer can claim rights, creating a clear in-text contradiction regarding ownership and potentially infringing the Client's exclusive property rights as defined in the contradicted text. This aligns with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction
